summary: Typosquat of tailwind-merge executing obfuscated payload at module load
details: The package name `tailwindcss-merge` is a one-segment edit of the widely used `tailwind-merge` package and mirrors that library's public API (`twMerge`, `twJoin`, `extendTailwindMerge`, `fromTheme`, `createTailwindMerge`), with README examples identical to the legitimate library's usage. The entrypoint `src/index.ts` ends with a side-effect import `import './lib/lib.min.js';` that has no functional relationship to the advertised class-merging utility. `src/lib/lib.min.js` stashes Node's `require` and `module` on the global object (`global.r = require`, `global.m = module`), runs a custom positional-shuffle decoder (`YWG`) over two large opaque string literals, retrieves the `Function` constructor via the decoded property name `constructor`, and invokes `Function('', <decoded body>)(7942)` to execute arbitrary code at module load. This is the canonical obfuscated-eval shape: decoded bytes from opaque blobs gain full Node.js capability the moment any consumer requires the package, while the typosquat name targets developers who intended to install `tailwind-merge`. The obfuscation (no comments, no source map, custom shuffle decoder, sole opaque sibling among otherwise plain TypeScript sources) confirms intent to hide installer-harm behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
